name: AMPULLARY TUMOR
definition: benign / malignant tumors arising from glandular epithelium of ampulla of Vater AMPULLARY ADENOCARCINOMA rare malignancy arising in ampullary complex distal to confluence of the pancreatic duct uncommon premalignant lesion

demographics.Histo: (a) dysplastic epithelium in glandular / villous structures of tubular / villous
demographics.Associated with: familial adenomatous polyposis syndromes (eg, familial polyposis coli,

distribution: filling defect / focal stricture at distal end of dilated CBD
discriminating_features: tumor often inapparent ← small size (more readily visualized with well-distended duodenum)
indentation of duodenal lumen at papilla of Vater with filling defect > 1.5 cm
surface irregularity + deep barium-filled crevices in villous tumor
dilatation of most distal segment of common bile duct
stenosis ← circumferential tumor growth around ampulla / desmoplastic reaction

general: tumor often inapparent ← small size (more readily visualized with well-distended duodenum)
indentation of duodenal lumen at papilla of Vater with filling defect > 1.5 cm
surface irregularity + deep barium-filled crevices in villous tumor
dilatation of most distal segment of common bile duct
stenosis ← circumferential tumor growth around ampulla / desmoplastic reaction
± pancreatic dilatation = "double-duct" sign (may be absent if tumor small / accessory
CT: discrete nodular mass with irregular filling defect at distal margin of pancreaticobiliary
enhancement on arterial + portal venous phase
MR: isointense relative to adjacent duodenal wall on T1WI
hypointense relative to surrounding duodenum at arterial phase imaging
improved detection of ampullary carcinoma with DWI
filling defect / focal stricture at distal end of dilated CBD
"double-duct" sign = dilatation of biliary + pancreatic ducts (in 52%):
marked abrupt dilatation of distal CBD / pancreatic duct
NO signs of pancreatitis / pancreatic mass / stone
NO "double-duct" sign if separate duodenal openings for biliary and pancreatic ducts
soft-tissue mass > 1 cm with irregular margin of ampulla
extrahepatic biliary duct + pancreatic duct dilatation

clinical.Prognosis: 28-70% 5-year survival for ampullary carcinomas (depending on stage)
